subcommands.name: addmedia
subcommands.description: add photos, live photos, videos, or contacts to the library of a device

subcommands.name: boot
subcommands.description: boot a device or device pair

options.description: specify the architecture to use when booting the simulator (eg: 'arm64' or 'x86_64')
disables the given launchd job. Multiple jobs can be disabled by passing multiple flags
enables the given launchd job when it would normally be disabled. Multiple jobs can be enabled by passing multiple flags

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description: clone an existing device

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: create a new device

states.name: device-type-id
states.description: a valid available device type. Find these by running 'xcrun simctl list devicetypes'. Examples: ('iPhone X', 'com.apple.CoreSimulator.SimDeviceType.iPhone-X')

states.name: runtime-id
states.description: a valid and available runtime. Find these by running 'xcrun simctl list runtimes'. If no runtime is specified the newest runtime compatible with the device type

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete specified devices, unavailable devices, or all devices

states.name: unavailable
states.description: specifying unavailable will delete devices that are not supported by the current Xcode SDK

subcommands.name: diagnose
subcommands.description: collect diagnostic information and logs

options.description: do NOT show the resulting archive in a Finder window upon completion
run all diagnostics with no timeout. It ignores the --timeout value if it is specified
specify a duration (in seconds) to wait for the log collection before timeout
specify the output directory. If not provided the temporary directory is used
do not create an archive, leave the collected files uncompressed
include all device logs, even for non-booted devices
include booted device(s) data directory. Warning: May include private information, app data containers, and increases the size of the archive! Default is NOT to
collect diagnostics only from the specified device. This option may be specified more than once to consider multiple devices. The --all-logs option causes all -

subcommands.name: erase
subcommands.description: erase a device's contents and settings

states.name: all
states.description: specifying all will erase all existing devices

subcommands.name: get_app_container
subcommands.description: print the path of the installed app's container

states.name: container
states.description: optionally specify the container. Defaults to app. app The .app bundle data The application's data container groups The App Group containers <group identifier>

subcommands.name: getenv
subcommands.description: print an environment variable from a running device

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: prints the usage for a given subcommand

subcommands.name: icloud_sync
subcommands.description: trigger iCloud sync on a device

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: install an app on a device

subcommands.name: install_app_data
subcommands.description: install an xcappdata package to a device, replacing the current contents of the container

subcommands.name: io
subcommands.description: set up a device IO operation

subcommands.name: enumerate
subcommands.description: lists all available IO ports and descriptor states

options.description: poll after enumeration

subcommands.name: poll
subcommands.description: polls all available IO ports for events

subcommands.name: recordVideo
subcommands.description: records the display to a QuickTime movie at the specified file or url

options.description: specifies the codec type: 'h264' or 'hevc'. Default is 'hevc'
iOS: supports 'internal' or 'external'. Default is 'internal'. tvOS: supports only 'external' watchOS: supports only 'internal'
for non-rectangular displays, handle the mask by policy: ignored: The mask is ignored and the unmasked framebuffer is saved. alpha: Not supported, but retained
force the output file to be written to, even if the file already exists

subcommands.name: screenshot
subcommands.description: saves a screenshot as a PNG to the specified file or url(use '-' for stdout)

options.description: can be 'png', 'tiff', 'bmp', 'gif', 'jpeg'. Default is png
iOS: supports 'internal' or 'external'. Default is 'internal'. tvOS: supports only 'external' watchOS: supports only 'internal'. You may also specify a port by
for non-rectangular displays, handle the mask by policy: ignored: The mask is ignored and the unmasked framebuffer is saved. alpha: The mask is used as premulti

subcommands.name: keychain
subcommands.description: manipulate a device's keychain

subcommands.name: add-root-cert
subcommands.description: add a certificate to the trusted root store

subcommands.name: add-cert
subcommands.description: add a certificate to the keychain

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: reset the keychain

subcommands.name: launch
subcommands.description: launch an application by identifier on a device

options.description: block and print the application's stdout and stderr to the current terminal. Signals received by simctl are passed through to the application. (Cannot be combin
block and print the application's stdout and stderr to the current terminal via a PTY. Signals received by simctl are passed through to the application. (Cannot
redirect the application's standard output to a file
redirect the application's standard error to a file
terminate any running copy of the application. Note: Log output is often directed to stderr, not stdout

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list available devices, device types, runtimes, or device pairs

options.description: print as JSON
encode slashes without escapes in JSON output
more verbose output

subcommands.name: location
subcommands.description: control a device's simulated location

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list available simulation scenarios

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: stop any running scenario and clear any simulated location

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: set the location to a specific latitude and longitude

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: run a simulated location scenario (use the list action to get a list of scenarios)

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: set the location to a series of waypoints specified as 'lat,lon' pairs, interpolating between them over time. At least two waypoints are required. Use '-' to re

subcommands.name: logverbose
subcommands.description: enable or disable verbose logging for a device. NOTE: You may need to reboot the affected device before logging changes will be effective

states.name: device
states.description: the device. If not provided all booted devices are affected

subcommands.name: openurl
subcommands.description: open a URL in a device

subcommands.name: pair
subcommands.description: create a new watch and phone pair

subcommands.name: pair_activate
subcommands.description: set a given pair as active

subcommands.name: pbcopy
subcommands.description: copy standard input onto the device pasteboard

subcommands.name: pbpaste
subcommands.description: print the contents of the device's pasteboard to standard output

subcommands.name: pbsync
subcommands.description: sync the pasteboard content from one pasteboard to another

options.description: causes simctl to use promise data for secondary types. simctl will continue to run to provide that promise data until something else replaces it on the pasteboa

subcommands.name: privacy
subcommands.description: grant, revoke, or reset privacy and permissions

states.name: bundle-identifier
states.description: the bundle identifier of the target application

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: send a simulated push notification

states.name: bundle-identifier
states.description: the bundle identifier of the target application If the payload file contains a 'Simulator Target Bundle' top-level key this parameter may be omitted. If both ar

states.name: json-file
states.description: path to a JSON payload or '-' to read from stdin. The payload must: - Contain an object at the top level. - Contain an 'aps' key with valid Apple Push Notificat

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description: rename a device

subcommands.name: runtime
subcommands.description: perform operations on runtimes

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: add a runtime disk image to the secure storage area. The image will be staged, verified, and mounted. When possible the image file will be cloned so no addition

options.description: remove the original file if the image is added successfully. If the image cannot be staged or the add fails the original is not removed
print the UUID of the new image then exit, do not wait on the results of the add operation

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: delete a simulator runtime from the secure storage area. If runtime is a disk image any booted simulators are shutdown and the disk is unmounted first. Use the

options.description: delete images not used within the past <days> days
print what images would be deleted without actually deleting anything

subcommands.name: verify
subcommands.description: re-verify the signature of a given runtime

options.description: print more verbose output
print as JSON

subcommands.name: match list
subcommands.description: list the SDK build to runtime build mapping rules for the selected Xcode. Preferred means the runtime was either bundled with Xcode, exactly matched your SDK ve

options.description: verbose mode. Includes the full preferred build map, user override map, and known SDK names
print as JSON

subcommands.name: match set
subcommands.description: override the SDK to runtime build mapping. This controls which build of a given runtime Xcode will prefer for building and running when using that SDK. This mat

options.description: clear the override for the given SDK and revert to default behavior
explicitly specify the SDK build, eg for an Xcode other than the selected Xcode

subcommands.name: shutdown
subcommands.description: shutdown a device

subcommands.name: spawn
subcommands.description: spawn a process by executing a given executable on a device

subcommands.name: status_bar
subcommands.description: set or clear status bar overrides

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: list existing overrides

subcommands.name: clear
subcommands.description: clear all existing status bar overrides

subcommands.name: override
subcommands.description: set status bar override values, according to these flags. You may specify any combination of these flags (at least one is required)

options.description: set the date or time to a fixed value. If the string is a valid ISO date string it will also set the date on relevant devices
if specified must be one of 'hide', 'wifi', '3g', '4g', 'lte', 'lte-a', 'lte+', '5g', '5g+', '5g-uwb', or '5g-uc'
if specified must be one of 'searching', 'failed', or 'active'
if specified must be 0-3
if specified must be one of 'notSupported', 'searching', 'failed', or 'active'
if specified must be 0-4
set the cellular operator/carrier name. Use '' for the empty string
if specified must be one of 'charging', 'charged', or 'discharging'
if specified must be 0-100

subcommands.name: terminate
subcommands.description: terminate an application by identifier on a device

subcommands.name: ui
subcommands.description: get or Set UI options

subcommands.name: appearance
subcommands.description: when invoked without arguments prints the current user interface appearance style: light The Light appearance style. dark The Dark appearance style. unsupported

subcommands.name: increase_contrast
subcommands.description: when invoked without arguments prints whether the Increase Contrast mode is currently enabled: enabled Increase Contrast is enabled. disabled Increase Contrast

subcommands.name: content_size
subcommands.description: when invoked without arguments prints the current preferred content size category, from the following possible values:

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: uninstall an app from a device

subcommands.name: unpair
subcommands.description: unpair a watch and phone pair

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: upgrade a device to a newer runtime
